title: Paychex: Opportunities With This Market Leading Tech Business
text: Paychex has a highly attractive business model with consistent growth and impressive margins. The business has developed a market-leading suite of services that create sticky demand. PAYX has the potential for further growth through product development, upselling/cross-selling, M&A, and international expansion. The company's recent performance has been good, implying resilience against market conditions.

title: August Job Growth Among Small Businesses Unchanged from July
text: ROCHESTER, N.Y.--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $PAYX #employmenttrends--According to the Paychex | IHS Markit Small Business Employment Watch, the national Small Business Jobs Index — which measures the rate of small business job growth in the U.S. — is essentially flat at 99.10, a decrease of 0.04% in August. title: Paychex Named Largest 401(k) Recordkeeper in the U.S. for 13th Consecutive Year
text: ROCHESTER, N.Y.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--For the 13th year in a row, Paychex, Inc. has earned PLANSPONSOR magazine's distinction of being the nation's largest 401(k) recordkeeper by total number of plans. Paychex, Inc., a leading provider of integrated human capital management software solutions for human resources, employee benefits, insurance services, and payroll, serves nearly 110,000 plans. title: Paychex: Hold This Innovative Payroll Leader With Positive Net Income Growth
text: Paychex gets a neutral / hold rating today, in line with the consensus from Wall Street and the Seeking Alpha quant system. Positives: YoY net income growth, capital and liquidity strength, dividend yield above sector average. Headwinds: valuation above sector average, share price well above 200 day moving average.

title: Job Market Remains Stable, Wage Growth Drops Below Four Percent
text: ROCHESTER, N.Y.--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $PAYX #employmenttrends--According to the Paychex | IHS Markit Small Business Employment Watch, hourly earnings growth (3.95%) has dipped below four percent for the first time since 2021, and one-month annualized growth in hourly earnings remains below four percent for the fourth consecutive month.
